title: cuphea thymoides
intro: Cuphea thymoides, also known as the wild basil or Mexican basil, is a plant species belonging to the family Lythraceae. It is native to Mexico and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Cuphea thymoides can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a versatile herbal remedy.
